riskFloorReasons: safety-invariant proximity: src/monitoring/SessionReaper.ts matches session reaper
new capability: new exported class / subsystem added

causalAutopsy.notes: The post-transfer closeout decision in SessionReaper keyed solely on the topic's ownership record (who SHOULD hold the topic) and never verified that the destination machine actually holds a LIVE session. The gap was always present but only surfaced as a visible bug when an ownership record went stale (owner still names the remote machine after that machine already finished) — the reaper then looped trying to terminate the one local session still doing real work. Fixed by liveness-gating the closeout: it proceeds only when the remote owner is confirmed live; owner-dead / unknown / unresolvable all resolve to the SAFE WITHHOLD direction (fail-closed), with a narrow recent-user-message bypass that re-checks every other KEEP-guard so a live-working session is never killed.
